c.NotebookApp.ip = '0.0.0.0'

c.NotebookApp.certfile = {repr(os.path.join(config_dir, 'jupyter.crt'))}
c.NotebookApp.keyfile = {repr(os.path.join(config_dir, 'jupyter.key'))}
c.NotebookApp.allow_root = True
''')

Since netstat/ss aren't available, I can use Python to check if the port is open.

First, kill any existing jupyter processes, then start it with nohup to keep it running.

```bash
pkill -f jupyter; sleep 2 && nohup /opt/miniswea/bin/jupyter-notebook --config ~/.jupyter/jupyter_notebook_config.py --no-browser --port 8888 --ip 0.0.0.0 --allow-root > /tmp/jupyter.log 2>&1 &
sleep 8
python3 -c "
import socket
s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
result = s.connect_ex(('0.0.0.0', 8888))
if result == 0:
    print('Port 8888 is open')
else:
    print('Port 8888 is not open')
s.close()
"
